meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about your lack of portfolio. Use this advanced SaaS starter kit guide to launch your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth—revenue is the only portfolio that matters. By utilizing a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you bypass the friction of boilerplate code, allowing you to deploy functional, paid-ready features in hours. Focus on solving a specific pain point, shipping fast, and iterating based on real user data.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank repository, paralyzed by the 'no portfolio' syndrome. You believe that without a showcase of polished, complex apps, no one will trust your vision. But here is the brutal truth: customers don't buy your history; they buy the solution to their current, agonizing problem.

It is time to stop building for your ego and start building for your bottom line. By leveraging an enterprise-grade SaaS starter kit, you compress months of infrastructure work into a single afternoon. This isn't just about writing code; it's about weaponizing speed-to-market to secure your first paying user before your competitors even finish their setup.

steps.title: Architect the MVP Scope
steps.description: Define the absolute minimum feature set that provides value. Avoid feature creep by focusing exclusively on the primary conversion path.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a lean MVP for a [Type of SaaS] that solves [Specific Problem]. Prioritize user auth, Stripe integration, and one core value-add feature. Provide a modular architecture schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project roadmap with a focus on core conversion paths only'

steps.title: Scaffold with Starter Kit
steps.description: Initialize your project using a battle-tested stack. We prioritize type safety and scalability from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a directory structure for a production-ready SaaS using Next.js 15, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include folder structures for middleware, API routes, and database models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding based on the provided architecture schema'

steps.title: Implement Auth & Billing
steps.description: Never build authentication or billing systems from scratch. These are commodities that should be outsourced to mature providers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft the integration logic for Supabase Auth and Stripe Webhooks. Ensure error handling for subscription status is robust.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement authentication flow and stripe webhook handler for subscription status updates'

steps.title: Build the Core Value Loop
steps.description: Focus your coding effort on the unique logic that differentiates your product from the competition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for [Unique Feature]. Ensure it is decoupled from the UI and follows clean architecture principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core business logic module and write unit tests for the primary function'

steps.title: Deployment & Analytics
steps.description: Deploy to production immediately. If you aren't tracking user behavior, you aren't building a business; you're building a hobby.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Vercel deployment settings and integrate PostHog event tracking for user onboarding milestones.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure production deployment settings and initialize analytics tracking hooks'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Time to First Dollar' over 'Time to Perfect Code'. Clean code is a luxury; revenue is a necessity.
Use mock data for UI development to keep your velocity high while waiting for real database schema finalization.
Never build custom OAuth providers; use Clerk or Supabase to save 40+ hours of security engineering.
Implement feature flags from day one to deploy broken code safely and test features on live users without risking the main build.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a living document; constantly prune unused dependencies to keep your bundle size lean.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, shift your focus entirely to distribution. Reach out to 50 potential users in your target niche, gather feedback, and iterate on your core value proposition.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'no portfolio' excuse kill your SaaS dreams. By using the right tools and a disciplined engineering mindset, you can launch a profitable product in a fraction of the time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
